message:
feat(notifications): NotificationSender trait Cx-first extension (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Extends `NotificationSender` trait with a `send_with_cx` default
method and routes `NotificationPipeline::handle_detection_with_cx`
through a new `dispatch_payload_with_cx` helper so each sender's
cancellable I/O honors caller cancellation.

Scope:

1. crates/frankenterm-core/src/notifications.rs (+35 src)

   a. `NotificationSender::send_with_cx` default method.

      Signature:
        fn send_with_cx<'a>(
            &'a self,
            _cx: &'a crate::cx::Cx,
            payload: &'a NotificationPayload,
        ) -> NotificationFuture<'a>

      Default delegates to `send(payload)`. Concrete implementations
      with a Cx-aware transport (webhook HTTP client, SMTP sender,
      etc.) SHOULD override this method to propagate cx through the
      transport's cancellable I/O.

      Follows the `WeztermInterface` trait-level Cx-first pattern
      (ticks 27-29): default delegates to legacy, overrides only
      where a Cx-aware implementation exists.

   b. `NotificationPipeline::dispatch_payload_with_cx` internal helper.

      Cx-first counterpart to `dispatch_payload`. Iterates senders
      calling `sender.send_with_cx(cx, payload)`. Used exclusively
      by `handle_detection_with_cx`.

   c. `handle_detection_with_cx` now calls `dispatch_payload_with_cx`
      (previously used the ambient `dispatch_payload`). Closes the
      ambient-Cx gap at the sender-dispatch boundary in the tick 25
      Cx-first pipeline entry point.

   Legacy `dispatch_payload` and `send()` preserved; strictly
   additive.

Impact: the NotificationPipeline Cx-first chain is now cx-aware
end-to-end from call site through mute-check RwLock (tick 25) to
each sender's transport (this commit). No ambient-Cx hops.

Verification (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target):

  * cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib notifications::: 46/46 pass
  * cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --lib -D warnings: clean
  * rustfmt --check: clean

Leaves ft-xbnl0.2.3 still in_progress.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
